meta.name: Share time-limited preview links with UploadToURL, SendGrid, and Google Sheets

parameters.content:
## Share time-limited preview links with UploadToURL, SendGrid, and Google Sheets
The Problem: Standard email attachments stay accessible forever, creating security risks and offering no tracking for sensitive agency drafts.
The Solution: A "burner link" delivery system that hosts files via UploadToURL, emails them via SendGrid, and automatically updates the link status to "Expired" in Google Sheets after a set time.

⚙️ How it Works
Webhook: Receives a file (Binary or URL) and an expiry duration (e.g., 24 hours).

UploadToURL: Hosts the asset instantly and returns a public CDN link.

SendGrid: Emails the branded preview link to the client with an expiry notice.

Wait & Expire: The workflow pauses for the set duration, then updates Google Sheets to mark the link as expired.

🔐 Credentials & Setup
Node: Install n8n-nodes-uploadtourl via Community Nodes.

APIs: UploadToURL, SendGrid, and Google Sheets.

Variables: Set GSHEET_SPREADSHEET_ID and DEFAULT_EXPIRY_HOURS

## 1 — Upload & link generation

**Webhook → Validate → Has Remote URL? → Upload to URL (×2) → Extract CDN URL → Generate Link Record**

Validates recipient email format and expiry hours (1–168 range). UploadToURL hosts the file via the native community node. The Generate node creates a unique `token` (hex ID), computes `expiresAt` from `expiryHours`, and assembles the full delivery record before anything is sent.

## 2 — Log & send

**Sheets - Log Active Link → SendGrid - Send Preview Email**

Google Sheets records the link immediately as `active` before the email is sent — so there's always a record even if SendGrid fails. The email is an HTML-formatted preview with the file name, expiry time, project name, and a clear CTA button. The agency receives a BCC copy.

## 3 — Wait & expire

**Wait Node (configurable duration) → Sheets - Mark Expired → SendGrid - Expiry Notice**

The Wait node pauses this execution instance for the exact `expiryHours` value. After resuming, the sheet row is updated to `expired` with an `Expired At` timestamp. Two emails fire: a polite expiry notice to the client, and a delivery summary to the agency confirming the link lifecycle is complete.

## ⚙️ Wait node note

This workflow uses n8n's **Wait** node to pause execution between send and expiry. Each webhook submission runs as its own execution instance — multiple links can be in-flight simultaneously. Requires n8n to be running (cloud or self-hosted with executions enabled). The wait duration is set dynamically from the `expiryHours` field in the webhook payload.

nodes.name: Generate Link Record & Email HTML
nodes.notes: Generates a unique hex token and expiry timestamp. Pre-builds all three HTML emails (preview, client expiry notice, agency summary) in one pass so downstream nodes just reference the pre-rendered strings.

nodes.name: Sheets - Log Active Link
nodes.notes: Logs the link record BEFORE sending the email — ensures a row always exists even if SendGrid fails.

nodes.name: SendGrid - Send Preview Email
nodes.notes: Sends a branded HTML preview email to the client with expiry notice. Agency email is BCC'd for records.

nodes.name: Respond to Webhook
nodes.notes: Returns immediately after the email is sent — before the Wait node fires. The caller gets confirmation without waiting 24 hours for the workflow to complete.

nodes.name: Wait - Until Link Expires
nodes.notes: Pauses this execution instance for the exact expiry duration. Set dynamically from expiryHours. n8n cloud persists the execution automatically; self-hosted requires execution persistence enabled.

nodes.name: SendGrid - Agency Summary Email
nodes.notes: Sends the agency a full lifecycle summary: token, file, recipient, sent time, expired time, and CDN URL.
